description: Hand-curated vicinal-3J validation corpus for the opt-in Karplus refinement of the Week-40 multiplet J-coupling layer (jcoupling_prediction.predict_proton_couplings_from_smiles(..., use_karplus=True)). Each entry pairs a SMILES with the literature-known DIAGNOSTIC MAXIMUM vicinal 3J(HCCH) a chemist would report for that system, plus a per-entry tolerance that reflects the documented limits of the GENERIC three-term Karplus relation (A=7.76, B=-1.10, C=1.40): it caps near 10.26 Hz at 180 degrees, so it under-predicts pure-hydrocarbon diaxial couplings (~11-13 Hz) by 1-2 Hz, and it ignores the electronegativity/orientation corrections that Haasnoot-Altona adds for sugars. The harness compares the predictor's MAXIMUM aliphatic_vicinal_karplus coupling against expected_max_vicinal_j_hz. This is a semi-quantitative DISCRIMINATION test (locked diaxial vs mobile/averaged), not a sub-Hz prediction claim. NOTE on predictor behaviour: the refinement averages each fixed H-pair dihedral UNWEIGHTED across the ETKDGv3+MMFF conformer ensemble, so a large diaxial coupling is recovered only for COVALENTLY/RIGIDLY locked rings (fused bicyclics, strong-preference pyranose chairs) -- thermodynamically anchored monocycles (e.g. 4-tert-butylcyclohexanol) ring-flip in the ensemble and their diaxial washes out, so they are intentionally excluded from the locked set.
source: Literature 1H-1H vicinal coupling constants from Pretsch/Buhlmann/Affolter (Structure Determination of Organic Compounds, 5th ed.), Silverstein/Webster/Kiemle (Spectrometric Identification of Organic Compounds, 8th ed.), Friebolin (Basic One- and Two-Dimensional NMR Spectroscopy, 5th ed.), and standard carbohydrate NMR references (e.g. anomeric/ring 3J for the beta-D-pyranoses).

fixtures.literature_note: Ring-fusion methines are rigidly locked diaxial (no ring flip is possible across the trans ring fusion); axial-axial 3J ~ 11-12 Hz. The generic Karplus 180-degree cap (10.26 Hz) structurally under-predicts pure-hydrocarbon diaxial by ~1 Hz -- the residual is the documented cap limitation, hence the 2.5 Hz tolerance.
fixtures.notes: Anchor case: a TRUE covalent lock. Every conformer keeps the same H-pairs diaxial, so the unweighted per-pair average stays high (~10.05 Hz observed).

fixtures.literature_note: All ring substituents equatorial in the strongly preferred 4C1 chair, so H1-H2/H2-H3/H3-H4/H4-H5 are all diaxial with 3J ~ 7.9-9.9 Hz (the ~9-9.5 Hz diaxials are the diagnostic large couplings). Generic Karplus slightly over-/under-predicts electronegative-substituent couplings (its Haasnoot-Altona blind spot), absorbed by the 2.0 Hz tolerance.
fixtures.notes: Anchor case: the 4C1 chair is strongly preferred and ETKDG/MMFF reproduces it, so the ring behaves as rigidly locked (~9.59 Hz observed).

fixtures.literature_note: Cyclohexane-hexol locked in one dominant chair (five equatorial OH, one axial at C2); the axial-axial ring couplings are ~9-10 Hz. Diagnostic large diaxial ~9.9 Hz.
fixtures.notes: Anchor case: rigid all-chair cyclitol (~9.85 Hz observed).

fixtures.literature_note: Galacto configuration (axial 4-OH) means H3-H4 and H4-H5 are small (ax-eq ~3.5 Hz / eq-ax ~1 Hz), but H1-H2 (~7.8 Hz) and H2-H3 (~9.9 Hz) remain diaxial. Diagnostic large diaxial ~9.9 Hz (H2-H3). Generic Karplus under-predicts here (~8.5 Hz observed) -- a clear example of the electronegativity blind spot, hence the wider 2.5 Hz tolerance.
fixtures.notes: Anchor case + honest stress test: shows the generic-Karplus under-prediction on a hydroxylated diaxial.

fixtures.literature_note: Rapid chair-chair ring flip at room temperature averages Jaa (~11-13 Hz) with Jae/Jee (~3-4 Hz) to a single time-averaged 3J ~ 6.8 Hz. The predictor's unweighted conformer average reproduces this (~7.14 Hz observed).
fixtures.notes: Mobile control: confirms NO spurious large diaxial is emitted for a freely flipping ring.

fixtures.literature_note: Unlike rigid trans-decalin, cis-decalin interconverts between two equivalent chair-chair forms, so its couplings are conformationally averaged to ~6-7 Hz. Direct contrast to the trans isomer.
fixtures.notes: Mobile control + diastereomer discriminator: cis (~6.83 Hz) vs trans (~10.05 Hz) is the headline locked-vs-mobile pair.

fixtures.literature_note: Freely rotating acyclic C-C bond; the rotamer-averaged vicinal 3J(CH2-CH2) is ~7.3 Hz (the canonical 'free rotation' value).
fixtures.notes: Acyclic control: rotamer averaging lands on the generic ~7 Hz value (~7.12 Hz observed).

fixtures.literature_note: CH3-CH2 vicinal 3J ~ 7.0 Hz (textbook freely-rotating ethyl coupling).
fixtures.notes: Acyclic control: classic ethyl 3J (~6.50 Hz observed).
